source_note: Derived from one (WFH + dehumidifier) household's real Octopus HH data over a 12-month window. Statistics only — no consumption values in this file, no specific dates that could identify the household. Sanitised version of the full extraction output, which lives locally only. See test-data-strategy.md §C and the corresponding noise-calibration design doc for methodology.

measurement_noise._note: Hardcoded per UK smart-meter spec (~1-2% measurement noise). Not data-calibrated.

behavioural_noise._hh_residual_autocorr_lag1_note: Per-day median (P50) of within-day lag-1 autocorrelation. RECOMMENDED to override per-archetype rather than use this default. Synthesiser archetype config should include optional `noise_overrides.hh_residual_autocorr_lag1` to override.

hh_residual_autocorr_lag1_distribution._note: Per-day within-day autocorr distribution from the calibration household. Demos can pick values in [P10, P90] within this empirical envelope. Higher autocorr = smoother noise (more consistent activity backbone). Lower = more variable noise (more discrete events / sharp transitions). Suggested per-archetype values are in test-data-strategy.md §E.

behavioural_noise._note_on_cv: daily_residual_cv computed on total elec after elec~gas linear detrending (proxy for weather detrending). daily_activity_cv computed on activity elec (total minus 24/7 baseline). For synthesiser use, daily_activity_cv isolates the human-activity variance signal — but treat as a lower bound, since the calibration household has a stable dehumidifier-driven baseline that may understate variance versus higher-event archetypes (e.g. families).

weekday_weekend_elec_ratio_calibration_h._warning: NOT transferable across archetypes. The calibration household is WFH-based; its weekday/weekend ratio is atypical (weekdays > weekends because work-at-home loads dominate weekdays, weekends often involve being out). Recorded for reference only. Demos must set this per archetype based on the occupancy schedule chosen for that demo.

schedule_jitter._note: Hardcoded default — modern timers are precise (~5 min) but occupants frequently override (cold morning, etc.), giving effective SD of ~15 min. Not data-calibrated (cumulative-fraction approach conflates weather effects with timing variance).

holiday_weeks._note: Aggregate count and mean duration of true-absence events from the calibration household. Specific dates are not included in this file. Synthesiser uses these aggregates to inject N absence runs of mean length D into demo CSVs, with M3 absence-detection then picking them up during round-trip validation.

baseline_signature_calibration_household._warning: Per-HH 24/7 baseline (dehumidifier + fridge + standby) from the calibration household. NOT transferred to demos — each demo sets its own baseline parametrically per archetype config (gas_hot_water_kwh_per_day + elec_baseload_kwh_per_day + appliance events). Recorded here for methodological reference only.
